dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for cardiac arrest at a [CITY] residence.
Hi, I'm Alex with the rescue squad. Can someone describe what happened here?
We found him not breathing in bed. He looked unconscious and had no pulse.
Does he respond to voice, touch, or any stimuli?
His eyes were open, but he wasn't moving or reacting. He usually doesn't respond due to cerebral palsy.
Any verbal response when you spoke to him?
None. It's his baseline condition-he doesn't move independently and never initiates movement.
Airway appears compromised due to unresponsiveness and need for assisted breathing.
No spontaneous breathing-will need BVM ventilations to maintain oxygenation.
Carotid pulse is absent. Skin is warm and dry with no cyanosis.
No palpable pulse-recording as zero.
No respiratory effort-will use assisted breathing via BVM.
Blood pressure is undetectable-recording as 0/0.
SpO2 cannot be obtained due to absence of spontaneous breathing and pulse.
Blood glucose is not obtainable as per our protocol.
EKG is showing asystole-flatline with no electrical activity.
What's his age and any known medical conditions?
He was fine 1.5 hours ago, but we found him unresponsive with abdominal distention and shallow breathing.
He has cerebral palsy, epilepsy, and mild asthma. He takes phenobarbital for seizures.
Taking phenobarbital long-term-no recent changes.
No known drug or food allergies.
Has a feeding tube, so I can't tell when he last consumed anything.
I returned to his room and found him unresponsive on the bed. He was breathing at 1.5 hours ago, but now he's not.
GCS is 3-eye 1, verbal 1, motor 1. Typical for his condition, but concerning for lack of change.
Any pain observed before unconsciousness?
I didn't see any. He was already unconscious when we found him.
Field impression is cardiac arrest and respiratory failure likely.
Starting CPR immediately-manual compressions at 100 to 120 per minute.
Assisting breaths with BVM and 15 LPM oxygen. Taking compressions now.
Activating AutoPulse for mechanical compressions-maintaining consistent rhythm now.
AUTO-PULSE is active. Checking rhythm-no change-still in asystole.
Inserting an oropharyngeal airway to manage airway during this phase.
OPA placed successfully. Ventilating still-airway clear and unobstructed.
Applying capnography to confirm airway placement and effectiveness.
Capnography not yet showing good waveform-re-checking airway depth.
Establishing intraosseous access at left humeral head. IV is not feasible.
IO access secured after site prep and cravat fixation. Ready for medication delivery.
Administering first epinephrine-1 mg intraosseously. Double-check with Partner.
Administering now and flushing line with saline. No improvement so far.
Inserting a King LT advanced airway now. Lubricated and correctly positioned.
King is in and capnography confirms placement. Good waveform visible.
Suctioning the airway-removed approximately 50 mL of pink, fluid.
Re-checking airway post-suction- waveform and chest rise are still adequate.
Suctioning again-removed small amount of fluid, airway remains clear.
Delivering second dose of epinephrine-1 mg IO. Pattern remains flat.
Re-suctioning airway with 18Fr catheter to ensure patency for effective ventilation.
Preparing third dose of epinephrine-single dose 1 mg per standing order.
Replacing capnography line-current one occluded. Waveform normalized within seconds.
Administering fourth 1 mg epinephrine dose-IO with saline flush.
Reached medical control. Continuing until 20 minutes or per MD order.
No pulse after 10 seconds. Still at 0.
No spontaneous respirations-still ventilated through artificial airway.
No blood pressure-still documented as 0/0.
SpO2 remains unobtainable-no pulse present.
Blood glucose not obtainable due to unavailability of device and lack of response.
Administered fourth round of epinephrine-no improvement in rhythm.
Per protocol, terminating resuscitation. Declared time of death at 23:03 per standing orders.
Notifying hospital of termination of efforts. Cause of death-refractory asystole.
Notifying family. Suspending all further interventions.
Resuscitation ceased as per medical control. No transport required.
Preparing patient for dignified release and family closure as instructed.
I understand. Thank you for your efforts and response.
Close all medical devices and documentation.
Scene processed and secured. Proceeding with clean-up and departure.
